methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $52M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Kinova Robotics is a late-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $51.6M to date. The comparable universe in Robotics & Manufacturing consists of 1 public company and 8 private peers, including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, no sufficiently narrow peer set exists at the same stage and valuation tier to support a median comparable analysis.

Given data constraints, the Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised ($51.6M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Late Stage progression, the analysis implies a valuation of $62M. Sensitivity around this central estimate—factoring typical dispersion in late-stage stage-step multiers—suggests a range from $53M (low) to $71M (high).

The analysis supports a valuation range of $53M to $71M, with $62M as the median point. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight peer comparables at Kinova's stage and the reliance on a single quantitative method. The range should be treated as illustrative pending access to transaction multiples or margin profiles from the broader peer set. Revenue run-rate and customer concentration data would materially improve confidence in the valuation.
